old_claim.note: Bronze Casting Molds provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency is retained because this node is scoped to worked native-copper fastener-like objects.

source_shape.source_locator: The Anatolian copper review anchors worked native-copper objects at Çayönü/Aşıklı; later bronze-casting molds are not required for early copper pins, hooks, and awls.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Early copper fastener-like objects are worked native-copper objects, not bronze castings.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Bronze casting molds are too late and too specific for early copper pins/hooks/awls.

ontology_before: Copper Rivets & Fasteners depended on Bronze Casting Molds.
ontology_after: The node is re-scoped to Copper Pins, Hooks & Fasteners and depends on copper working plus woodworking only.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed: Copper fastener-like objects must not directly depend on bronze casting molds unless narrowed to cast bronze fasteners.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to cast bronze rivets only.
A source shows the early copper pins/hooks/awls should not be grouped with fastener-like objects.
Bronze casting mold chronology is moved earlier with strong direct evidence.
short_reason: Worked native-copper pins/hooks/awls do not require later bronze molds.
